IsA: It is a holy icon of the highest stature within the Russian Orthodox Church.
It is a representation of the Virgin Mary.
It is a palladium of all of Russia and Rus'.
It is the Holy Protectress of Russia.
It is venerated by all Orthodox faithful.
HasA: It has a gold frame ornamented with many jewels.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by being a protector and patroness of the city of Kazan.
It is characterized by its pristine state when recovered.
It is characterized by its association with military victories.
It is characterized by its disappearance and subsequent theories about its fate.
It is characterized by its veneration in both Orthodox and Catholic Churches.
UsedFor: It is used for representing the Virgin Mary as the protector and patroness of the city of Kazan.
It is used for protecting all of Russia and Rus'.
It is used for veneration by Orthodox faithful.
It is used for displaying copies in cathedrals and churches.
It is used for invoking the Virgin Mary's help in repelling invasions.
LocatedIn: It is located in the Kazan Monastery of the Theotokos.
It is located on the site where the original icon of Our Lady of Kazan was found.
CreatedIn: It was created in the 16th century.
DiscoveredBy: It was discovered by a 10-year-old girl, Matrona.
DiscoveredIn: It was discovered under a destroyed house.
WrittenBy: It was written by Metropolitan Hermogenes.
Affects: It affects the veneration practices of the Russian Orthodox Church.
It affects the interpretation of historical events and tragedies in Russia.
It affects the naming of cathedrals and churches.
It affects the religious sentiment during times of invasion.
It affects the plans for an international pilgrimage centre.
PartOf: It is part of the veneration practices of the Russian Orthodox Church.
It is part of the history of Russia.
It is part of the religious heritage of the Eastern Orthodox Church.
It is part of the collection of icons housed in the Kazan Monastery of the Theotokos.
It is part of the history of the "Fátima image".
BelongsTo: It belongs to the Russian Orthodox Church.
It belongs to the greater Eastern Orthodox Church.
It belongs to the Kazan Monastery of the Theotokos.
It belongs to the history of Russia.
It belongs to the collection of holy icons.
KnownFor: It is known for being a holy icon of the highest stature.
It is known for its role as protector and patroness of Kazan.
It is known for its status as a palladium of all of Russia and Rus'.
It is known for its miraculous recovery in 1579.
It is known for its association with military victories against invaders.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Russian Orthodox Church.
It is associated with the city of Kazan.
It is associated with the protection of Russia.
It is associated with Metropolitan Hermogenes' chronicle.
It is associated with Russian military commanders Dmitry Pozharsky and Mikhail Kutuzov.
NamedAfter: It is named after the city of Kazan.
It is named after the Virgin Mary.
HeldIn: It was held in the Kazan Convent of the Theotokos.
It was held in the study of Pope John Paul II in the Vatican.
It was held in Fátima, Portugal.
It was held in the Annunciation Cathedral of the Kazan Kremlin.
It was held in the pavilion in New York.
About: It is about the Virgin Mary.
It is about the protection of Russia.
It is about miracles attributed to the Virgin Mary.
It is about the history of the Russian Orthodox Church.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the tradition of icons in the Eastern Orthodox Church.
It was influenced by the need for protection during invasions.
It was influenced by the events of the Russian Revolution.
It was influenced by the veneration practices of the Catholic Church.
It was influenced by the desire to return it to Russia.
Influenced: It influenced the veneration practices of the Russian Orthodox Church.
It influenced the interpretation of national tragedies.
It influenced the construction of cathedrals.
It influenced the morale of Russian soldiers.
It influenced the establishment of an international pilgrimage centre.
